"# Master Construction & Real Estate Website

A master corporate portal and application hub for a construction and real estate company, designed to integrate multiple micro-applications and deliver a premium, visually stunning user experience. The website will be built exactly to match the user's Figma designs.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Since this project requires matching Figma designs, we need you to upload or share the screenshots of the Figma pages.
> We also need to decide on the core technology stack (e.g., HTML/JS/CSS or a React/Vite application).

## Open Questions

> [!IMPORTANT]
> 1. **Figma Screenshots**: Please provide/upload the screenshots of each page from Figma so we can start matching the designs exactly.
> 2. **Technology Stack**: Do you prefer a standard, high-performance HTML/CSS/Vanilla JS setup, or a React + Vite Single Page Application (SPA)? A framework like React is highly recommended if you plan to integrate many interactive micro-applications later.
> 3. **Integrated Applications**: What kind of micro-applications or widgets are planned to be integrated (e.g., project portfolio viewer, booking/scheduling widgets, cost estimators, interactive maps, or client portals)? Knowing this will help us design the architecture to support them.
> 4. **Theme/Branding**: Do you have a specific color palette (e.g., dark mode preferred, warm tones, corporate blue, forest green/stone) or typography guidelines, or should we infer these directly from the Figma designs?

## Proposed Changes

We will initialize the project in the following subdirectory:
`C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\master-construction-portal`

### Project Initialization

#### [NEW] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
Initial landing page structure with standard meta tags, Google Fonts, and root elements.

#### [NEW] [style.css](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/style.cs
<truncated 807 bytes>

args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Master Construction & Real Estate Portal

A premium, interactive master dashboard matching the Figma screenshot provided. This site serves as a portal hosting multiple micro-applications.

## Implemented Features

1. **Dashboard Home**:
   - Left navigation sidebar matching the sidebar links and structure exactly.
   - Main dashboard header ("Good Evening, Pranshu" and status subtext).
   - 8 KPI cards with custom icons, statistics, and colored accent indicators:
     - My Tasks (8 / 3 due today) - Blue Checkbox Icon
     - Pending Approvals (5 / Requires action) - Orange Clock Icon
     - Team Workload (87% / Capacity) - Green Team Icon
     - Upcoming Shifts (12 / This week) - Purple Calendar Icon
     - Open Purchase Requests (6 / Awaiting review) - Red Cart Icon
     - Recent SOP Updates (4 / New this week) - Indigo Document Icon
     - Google Ads Performance ($4.2K / This month) - Green Chart Icon
     - Google Reviews Pending (8 / Need reply) - Gold Star Icon
2. **Master Application Integration**:
   - Rather than static links, the sidebar will dynamically switch views.
   - We will implement three fully interactive sub-applications:
     - **Tasks / Work Management**: A kanban board or checklist to add/update tasks.
     - **Purchase Requisition**: A form to submit new purchase requests and list current ones.
     - **Reputation Management**: A reviews panel to read and reply to pending Google Reviews.
3. **Rich Aesthetics**:
   - Modern typography using Google Fonts (Plus Jakarta Sans).
   - Clean, professional light mode matching the Figma screenshot, plus a premium dark mode toggle.
   - Micro-animations: Hover lift on cards, smooth page transitions, active nav states, and interactive SVG icon transitions.

## Proposed Files

All files will be located in the workspace directory:
`C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\master-construction-portal`

### Core Shell

#### [NEW] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/mas

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Checklist - Greens Global Dashboard

- [ ] Create core shell structure (`index.html`)
  - [ ] Left navigation sidebar (logo, title, nav items)
  - [ ] Main header (dashboard title, theme toggle, mobile toggle)
  - [ ] Main viewport container (`#main-viewport`)
- [ ] Implement design system (`style.css`)
  - [ ] Custom fonts (Plus Jakarta Sans)
  - [ ] CSS HSL variables for light and dark theme
  - [ ] Global styling, resets, layout setups
  - [ ] Styling for Sidebar and Main Header
  - [ ] Card styles (rounded, shadows, border, color tags)
  - [ ] Transitions, hover effects, and interactive animations
- [ ] Build core app state and controller (`app.js`)
  - [ ] Central state storage (active tab, tasks array, reviews array, purchase requests)
  - [ ] Theme toggler (persist theme preference in localStorage)
  - [ ] Navigation controller (update active item in sidebar, load view dynamically)
  - [ ] View rendering framework
- [ ] Build sub-view modules
  - [ ] Dashboard view (`views/dashboard.js`) - renders 8 KPI cards exactly matching screenshot
  - [ ] Tasks view (`views/tasks.js`) - interactive Kanban board / task list
  - [ ] Purchase view (`views/purchase.js`) - interactive form and list of requests
  - [ ] Reviews view (`views/reviews.js`) - interactive Google reviews panel
- [ ] Verification and Polish
  - [ ] Verify styling against the Figma screenshot
  - [ ] Test layout responsiveness (desktop, tablet, mobile)
  - [ ] Double-check interactive components for smooth animations and transitions
"
args.Description: "Create task checklist for dashboard implementation."

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully built the Greens Global Master Web Portal matching the layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma design. 

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and how to run and test it locally.

## Features Implemented

1. **Dashboard Home Layout**:
   - Sidebar containing the logo square **GG**, the text **Greens Global**, and navigation items exactly matching the Figma list.
   - Main dashboard grid featuring 8 KPI cards: **My Tasks**, **Pending Approvals**, **Team Workload**, **Upcoming Shifts**, **Open Purchase Requests**, **Recent SOP Updates**, **Google Ads Performance**, and **Google Reviews Pending**.
   - Colors and styling cues matching the Figma screenshot precisely (custom icons, rounded cards, muted helper text, accent colors).
2. **Interactive Sub-Applications**:
   - **Tasks / Work Management**: Renders a Kanban board with dynamic To Do, In Progress, and Completed columns. Includes a "New Task" form modal to add tasks and buttons to transition task states.
   - **Purchase Requisitions**: Renders a ledger of requisitions with "Approve" and "Reject" buttons, alongside a submission form. Approving items dynamically decrements the "Pending Approvals" count.
   - **Reputation Management**: Renders a Google Reviews board with star rating widgets and a response editor. Sending a reply decreases the "Google Reviews Pending" card count on the home screen.
3. **Theme & Responsiveness**:
   - Added a header toggler for Light and Dark theme (using professional HSL values).
   - Sidebar is fully collapsable on tablets and mobile devices with a slide-out drawer menu.

## Created Files

All codebase files are located in the project directory:
`C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\master-construction-portal`

- [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html): Layout shell, fonts, script linkages, and sidebar elements.
- [style.

   - **Purchase Requisitions**: Renders a ledger of requisitions with "Approve" and "Reject" buttons, alongside a submission form. Approving items dynamically decrements the "Pending Approvals" count.
   - **Reputation Management**: Renders a Google Reviews board with star rating widgets and a response editor. Sending a reply decreases the "Google Reviews Pending" card count on the home screen.
3. **Theme & Responsiveness**:
   - Added a header toggler for Light and Dark theme (using professional HSL values).

This removes the browser's local security (CORS) constraints for modules, letting you run it directly from your file system.

### How to Run the Website Instantly
1. Open your Windows **File Explorer**.
2. Navigate to your project folder:
   `C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\master-construction-portal`
3. **Double-click [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)**. It will open and run in your web browser immediately!

---

### What I Did
I updated the following files to support standard script loading:
* **[index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)**: Removed the module script flag (`type="module"`) and added sequential script loads for all views.
* **[app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)**: Removed the `import` statements at the top.
* **View Scripts ([dashboard.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/dashboard.js), [tasks.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/tasks.js), [purchase.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/purchase.js), [reviews.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/reviews.js))**: Removed `export` keywords and registered them globally.
* **[Walkthrough](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/brain/e2ce67ec-d36e-4d6a-b7d1-a7103081bfc8/walkthrough.md)**: Updated instructions to match this static file execution method.

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for both the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab) and **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

## Features Implemented

1. **Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)**:
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
   - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
   - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar" (clicking each dynamically changes the details card).
   - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Displays a table detailing each of the 6 active employee's stats (Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, etc.) with custom completed/overdue circular counts and colorful workload progress bars.
2. **Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)**:
   - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Replaced the Kanban layout with detailed, modern card rows. Each row displays the task ID (e.g. `TASK-001`), title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
   - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1) matching the state stats.
   - **Search & Filters**: Added a search input box that dynamically filters task rows by title, category, or assignee on keypress.
   - **Sub-navi
<truncated 1844 bytes>

### How to Run and View the Updates
1. Copy this file path link:
   ```text
   file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html
   ```
2. Paste it directly into your web browser's address bar.
3. Select **Manager Dashboard** or **Tasks / Work Management** in the left sidebar to test:

For more details, see the updated **[Walkthrough](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/brain/e2ce67ec-d36e-4d6a-b7d1-a7103081bfc8/walkthrough.md)**.

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), and **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

## Features Implemented

1. **Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)**:
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
   - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
   - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar" (clicking each dynamically changes the details card).
   - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Displays a table detailing each of the 6 active employee's stats (Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, etc.) with custom completed/overdue circular counts and colorful workload progress bars.
2. **Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)**:
   - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Replaced the Kanban layout with detailed, modern card rows. Each row displays the task ID (e.g. `TASK-001`), title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
   - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1) matching the state stats.
   - **Search & Filters**: Added a search input box that dynamically filters task rows by title, category, or assign
<truncated 3042 bytes>

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), and **IT Department** (5th tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

## Features Implemented

1. **Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)**:
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
   - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
   - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar" (clicking each dynamically changes the details card).
   - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Displays a table detailing each of the 6 active employee's stats (Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, etc.) with custom completed/overdue circular counts and colorful workload progress bars.
2. **Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)**:
   - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Replaced the Kanban layout with detailed, modern card rows. Each row displays the task ID (e.g. `TASK-001`), title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
   - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1) matching the state stats.
   - **Search & Filters**: Added a search input box that dynamically filters task rows

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), and **Operations** (7th tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

## Features Implemented

1. **Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)**:
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
   - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
   - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar" (clicking each dynamically changes the details card).
   - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Displays a table detailing each of the 6 active employee's stats (Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, etc.) with custom completed/overdue circular counts and colorful workload progress bars.
2. **Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)**:
   - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Replaced the Kanban layout with detailed, modern card rows. Each row displays the task ID (e.g. `TASK-001`), title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
   - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1) matching the state stats.
   - **Search & Filters**: Added a 
<truncated 5460 bytes>

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), and **Development** (8th tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

## Features Implemented

1. **Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)**:
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
   - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
   - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar" (clicking each dynamically changes the details card).
   - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Displays a table detailing each of the 6 active employee's stats (Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, etc.) with custom completed/overdue circular counts and colorful workload progress bars.
2. **Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)**:
   - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Replaced the Kanban layout with detailed, modern card rows. Each row displays the task ID (e.g. `TASK-001`), title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
   - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
   - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1) matching the state stats.
   - **S

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Marketing & Reputation View Merger

We will merge the **Marketing** (8th tab) and **Reputation Management** (10th tab) views into a single, cohesive, premium dashboard under the title **Marketing & Reputation**.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> - We will modify `index.html` to rename the navigation link to **Marketing & Reputation**, remove the separate **Reputation Management** navigation link, and replace the script reference for `views/reviews.js` with `views/marketing.js`.
> - We will create a new view module `views/marketing.js` containing the merged dashboard.
> - We will update `app.js` to redirect `reputation` to `marketing` in the router, add state objects for Marketing Campaigns, and manage the active tab/sub-tab configurations.
> - We will delete the obsolete `views/reviews.js` file.

## Open Questions

> [!NOTE]
> No outstanding questions exist. We will implement sub-tabs within the new merged view to allow toggling between:
> 1. **Google Ads Performance** (renders the Marketing dashboard, campaign table, and performance statistics)
> 2. **Reputation Management** (renders the review feed, AI reply suggestion box, ratings summary, and sub-tab review filters)

## Proposed Changes

### Core Shell & Navigation

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Change `<li class="nav-item" data-view="marketing">` to display `Marketing & Reputation`.
- Delete `<li class="nav-item" data-view="reputation">` navigation item.
- Remove `<script src="views/reviews.js"></script>` and add `<script src="views/marketing.js"></script>`.

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)
- Add `activeMarketingSubTab` (default: `'ads'`) and `activeReviewFilter` (default: `'all'`) to the global `state`.
- Include seed data for `marketingCampaigns` containing active/paused campaigns, impressions, clicks, conversions, aband
<truncated 3361 bytes>

args.CodeContent:
"# Task Checklist - Marketing & Reputation View Merger

- [ ] Update navigation sidebar shell (`index.html`)
  - [ ] Rename "Marketing" nav-item to "Marketing & Reputation"
  - [ ] Remove "Reputation Management" nav-item
  - [ ] Update scripts: remove `reviews.js` and add `marketing.js`
- [ ] Update central controller and routing (`app.js`)
  - [ ] Add initial sub-tab and filter states to `state`
  - [ ] Extend reviews list with platforms, properties, badges, and AI suggestion texts
  - [ ] Add Google Ads campaign metrics seed data
  - [ ] Update router: switch-case routes for `marketing` and redirect `reputation`
- [ ] Update Dashboard links (`views/dashboard.js`)
  - [ ] Update "Google Ads Performance" card to route to `marketing` view (Ads sub-tab)
  - [ ] Update "Google Reviews Pending" card to route to `marketing` view (Reputation sub-tab)
- [ ] Implement merged view component (`views/marketing.js`)
  - [ ] Create UI header with sub-tab buttons ("Google Ads Performance" and "Reputation Management")
  - [ ] Implement Google Ads sub-tab (8 KPI cards, campaign performance table, statistics footer)
  - [ ] Implement Reputation Management sub-tab (4 KPI cards, AI reply banner, sub-tab filter pills, reviews feed list)
  - [ ] Implement review filter behavior (All Reviews, Pending Reply, AI Suggested)
  - [ ] Add interactive "Review & Edit AI Reply" modal/form controls to draft, edit, and post replies (which updates the counts dynamically)
- [ ] Cleanup legacy files
  - [ ] Delete `views/reviews.js` from the workspace
- [ ] Verify functionality
  - [ ] Double click `index.html` and verify sidebar and router
  - [ ] Test the interactive sub-tabs and dashboard widgets
  - [ ] Verify edit and post workflows for AI replies
"

args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), and the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

---

## Features Implemented

### 1. Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)
- **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
- **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
- **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar".
- **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.

### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
- **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
- **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
- **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
- **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on keypress.
- **New Task Modal**: Allows creating new detailed card rows dynamically.

### 3. Knowledge Base / SOP (4th Tab)
- **SOP Category Cards**: Custom grids for IT Procedures, Accounting
<truncated 6394 bytes>

## 1. Marketing & Reputation View Merger (Completed)

### Changes Made:

* **Dashboard Widgets (`views/dashboard.js`)**: Linked the "Google Ads Performance" and "Google Reviews Pending" widgets directly to the correct sub-tabs of the merged view.
* **Component View (`views/marketing.js`)**: Implemented the dual-sub-tab layout featuring:
  1. **Google Ads Performance** (8 KPI cards, campaign performance table, statistics footer).
  2. **Reputation Management** (4 rating KPI cards, AI-Powered replies notice, filter pills, Google reviews feed, and an interactive inline AI response editor).
* **Cleanup**: Removed the obsolete `views/reviews.js` script.

---

## 2. Purchase Requisition (Cancelled)

* **Proposed Work**: Redesign the Purchase Requisition view to match the list-card layout.
* **Status**: **Cancelled by User**. As requested, the Purchase Requisition view remains unchanged in its current functional table-and-split state.

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections, and the newly built **Asset Management** (12th tab) section.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

---

## Features Implemented

### 1. Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)
- **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
- **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
- **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar".
- **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.

### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
- **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
- **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
- **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
- **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on keypress.
- **New Task Modal**: Allows creating new detailed card rows dynamically.

### 3. Knowledge Base / SOP (4th Tab)
- **SOP Cat

"# Implementation Plan - Admin & Access Control View

We will build the **Admin** (15th tab) view exactly as shown in your screenshots, providing full user management, SSO toggle configurations, and access logging tools.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> - We will create a new view module `views/admin.js` to render the Admin & Access Control dashboard.
> - We will modify `index.html` to load this new script file.
> - We will update `app.js` to initialize the global `state` with users lists, access logs data, and SSO switch states, and wire the `admin` routing case.
> - The status KPI cards (Total, Active, Admin, Logs) will be calculated dynamically from the state arrays, ensuring numbers match the screenshots exactly (8 Total, 7 Active, 1 Admin, 5 Logs) while remaining fully responsive to additions.

## Proposed Changes

### Configuration & State

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Add script inclusion `<script src="views/admin.js"></script>` in the scripts loading section.

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)
- Extend global `state` with:
  - `adminSettings` (SSO, MFA, Department-wise, Auto-import toggle variables).
  - `users` list containing John Mitchell, Sarah Johnson, Michael Chen, Emily Rodriguez, David Park, Lisa Thompson, Robert Kim, and Jennifer Lee, along with their roles, access levels, special access, status, and last login timestamps.
  - `accessLogs` list containing the 5 log rows.
- Wire the router case `case 'admin': window.renderAdmin(mainViewport, state, navigateTo); break;` to load the new view.

### Admin Dashboard Component

#### [NEW] [admin.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/admin.js)
- Implement `renderAdmin(container, state, navigateTo)`.
- **Header**: Title "Admin & Access Control", subtitle, "Import Users" and "+ Add User" buttons.

**Designing Admin View**

I'm currently focused on designing the Admin & Access Control view. It needs to be premium, so I'm considering custom styles, particularly for the toggle switches. Those screenshots look like iOS style toggles – round knobs, slide background – so that's where I'm starting. I'm double-checking the visual details of those switches.


**Refining CSS Switches**

I'm now diving into the CSS specifics for the admin view's switch toggles, looking at the existing `style.css` for a starting point. I've found existing classes for switch containers and sliders, but I'm planning to dynamically generate the CSS for custom, high-fidelity switches. args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections, the **Asset Management** (11th tab) section, and the newly built **Admin & Access Control** (12th tab) section.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

---

## Features Implemented

### 1. Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)
- **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
- **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
- **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar".
- **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.

### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
- **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
- **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
- **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
- **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on keypress.
- **New Task Modal**: Allows creating new detailed card rows dynamically.
\
<truncated 6692 bytes>

## Proposed Changes

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base / SOP** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections, the **Website Management** (10th tab) section, the **Asset Management** (11th tab) section, and the **Admin & Access Control** (12th tab) section.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

---

- **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).

- **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.

### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
- **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
- **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
- **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
- **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on keypress.
- **New Task Modal**: Allows creating new

I'm also considering how to provide functionality for adding, removing, and updating the links directly within the system, to enhance user experience.

I've decided to integrate a new "External Links" tab into the sidebar navigation. I'm adding it just before the existing "Admin" item in the `index.html` navigation list. **Implementing Link Management**

I'm now prioritizing the implementation of link management directly on the "External Links" page. My latest idea is an "Admin View: Enable Link Editing" toggle. This toggle would expose edit and delete buttons on each link card and reveal an "+ Add Link" button, creating a clean interface. This approach satisfies the admin interface requirement and offers an elegant user experience.


**Designing the Links Page**

I'm now creating the layout of the "External Links" page, with a focus on visual cleanliness and user-friendliness. I've defined the structure including a header with a toggle for "Admin Mode" and a design for category tabs. I'm building out a grid layout for links, with cards that clearly display link information. Finally, I've designed the necessary modals for link management, which will allow for adding and editing entries.

"# Implementation Plan - External Links View

We will implement the new **External Links** (13th tab) view to act as an internal start page, allowing dynamic categorization, search, click analytics, and admin-mode CRUD operations.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> - We will create a new view module `views/externalLinks.js` to render the External Links panel.
> - We will modify `index.html` to add the "External Links" navigation link and load the new script.
> - We will update `app.js` to define seed data for `externalLinks` in the global state, track click counts, and map routing.
> - We will add an "Admin Mode" toggle switch to the page header. Toggling "Admin Mode" on will display links edit/delete controls and a "+ Add Link" button, satisfying the requirement to edit links within the admin interface.

## Proposed Changes

### Configuration & State

  - Sage Intacct (Accounting, clicks: 85)
  - Asana (IT, clicks: 230)
  - CoConstruct (Operations, clicks: 64)
  - Autodesk Build (Development, clicks: 92)
  - HubSpot CRM (Marketing, clicks: 105)
- Wire router case `case 'external-links': window.renderExternalLinks(mainViewport, state, navigateTo); break;` in the routing switch.

### External Links View Component

#### [NEW] [externalLinks.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/externalLinks.js)
- Implement `renderExternalLinks(container, state, navigateTo)`.

args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough

We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Operations** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections, the **Website Management** (10th tab) section, the **Asset Management** (11th tab) section, the newly built **External Links** (12th tab) section, and the **Admin & Access Control** (13th tab) section.

Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.

Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.

---

## Features Implemented

### 1. Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)
- **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
- **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
- **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar".
- **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.

### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
- **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
- **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
- **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
- **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on key
<truncated 7061 bytes>

I have completed building the **External Links** dashboard view exactly as requested!

### What Was Accomplished
* **Sidebar Navigation**: Integrated the dashboard link in the sidebar list of [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html) and loaded the module script.
* **State & Router Integration**: Extended the global state in [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js) to store external links seed data and track click stats.
* **External Links Dashboard Component**: Created [externalLinks.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/externalLinks.js) featuring:
  * **Unified Grid Layout**: Renders clean system links containing names, domains, description paragraphs, and visit counters.
  * **Live Click Analytics**: Clicking any card logs a visit, increments the clicks count dynamically, and opens the system URL in a new window/tab.
  * **Categorization Filter Pills**: Displays filtering tags by department (Operations, Accounting, IT, Development, Marketing) or frequency of use (Most Frequent clicks count >= 100).
  * **Admin Edit Mode Toggle Switch**: Incorporates an iOS-slider switch on the top right. Toggling it on displays the "+ Add Link" button and "Edit" / "Delete" card actions.
  * **Link Management Modals**: Support adding new entries, updating existing metadata, or removing links directly.

Please check the updated [walkthrough.md](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/brain/e2ce67ec-d36e-4d6a-b7d1-a7103081bfc8/walkthrough.md) artifact for details on modifications and verification steps!

11: > - We will add an "Admin Mode" toggle switch to the page header. Toggling "Admin Mode" on will display links edit/delete controls and a "+ Add Link" button, satisfying the requirement to edit links within the admin interface.

thinking:
**Identifying the Overlap**

I've zeroed in on the visual issue the user reported. Analyzing the screenshot, it's clear the Material Symbols font is causing overlap with the sidebar labels. The "Construction" label highlights this problem particularly with `on` spilling over. I'm focusing on the font rendering causing the problem.


**Pinpointing the Cause**

I've realized the core issue is the Material Symbols font failing to load. The screenshot reveals this as the intended glyphs aren't displayed; instead, the fallback text ligatures ("construction", "engineering", etc.) render. The overlap results from the `<span>`'s styling (e.g., `overflow: visible;`) allowing the text to extend beyond its intended space and overlap adjacent labels.


**Investigating the Root Cause**

I've been drilling down on why the Material Symbols aren't rendering. It seems the issue is almost certainly due to the font not loading, either because of an internet connection problem or a blocked link to Google Fonts. I've confirmed the Google Fonts link is correct. Now I'm comparing this font loading technique to the Lucide icon strategy, which uses dynamic SVG loading that's self-contained. The user explicitly requested Material Symbols Outlined, but I'm thinking about a fallback, or even better, considering Lucide.


**Analyzing Font Loading Issues**

I'm now deep-diving into the font loading issue; I realize the user runs the portal offline, via `file:///`, thus, Google Fonts are not accessible. I am looking into how to make sure the icons are available regardless of internet status. This means the CDN approach used for Lucide icons is looking promising, as it loads via a script that might be cached.


**Prioritizing the Solution**

I've realized the optimal solution involves using the SVG versions of the requested Material Symbols icons. This eliminates font loading dependencies entirely. Inline SVGs from `engineering`, `corporate_fare`, and `construction` are self-contained and resolve both the offline issue and the overlap. I am now looking to source these SVG definitions. Lucide's icons are very similar.

content:
Created At: 2026-05-25T13:46:44Z
Completed At: 2026-05-25T13:46:50Z
The search for ""engineering" "material symbols" svg path" returned the following summary:
To obtain the SVG path for the "engineering" icon from Google Material Symbols, you can follow these methods:

### 1. Download Directly from Google Fonts
The official and most reliable source for Material Symbols is the [Google Fonts Material Symbols library](https://fonts.google.com/icons).
*   **Search:** Navigate to the site and search for "engineering."
*   **Select:** Click on the icon to open its details panel.
*   **Download:** You can download the icon as an **SVG file**. Once downloaded, you can open this file in any text editor (like VS Code or Notepad) to view the `<path>` data (the `d="..."` attribute).

### 2. Use a Third-Party SVG Library/Tool
If you prefer not to download files manually, several community-maintained tools provide direct access to the SVG paths:
*   **[All SVG Icons](https://allsvgicons.com/):** Allows you to browse, copy SVG code, or export as JSX.
*   **NPM Packages:** If you are working in a JavaScript/React environment, you can use packages like `@material-symbols/svg-400` which provide individual SVG files for each icon, allowing you to import them directly into your project without needing to copy raw path strings.

### Important Note on Material Symbols
Unlike older icon sets, **Material Symbols are variable icons**[1][2]. This means:
*   **Multiple Variants:** The "engineering" icon does not have just one path[3][4]. There are different paths for different **styles** (Outlined, Rounded, Sharp), **weights** (100–700), and **fill** states[2][4].

I have found and successfully fixed the issue.

### Root Cause of the Bug
When referencing Google Fonts' **Material Symbols** via the external CSS CDN, the browser requires active internet connectivity to download the font file. Since you are running this app locally/offline via `file://`, the external web font failed to load. 

When a ligature-based icon font fails to load, the browser falls back to rendering the actual text of the ligature (e.g., `"construction"`, `"engineering"`, and `"corporate_fare"`). Because these containers have a fixed width of `18px` (intended for square icons), the fallback text overflowed and overlapped with the navigation tab labels.

---

### Solution Implemented
To ensure the application is completely robust, runs offline, and never has unstyled text overlaps, I have done the following:

1. **Inlined Official Material Symbols SVGs**:
   - Replaced the font-based `<span class="material-symbols-outlined">` tags in [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html) with the exact, raw inline `<svg>` markup for the Google Font Outlined icons:
     - **Development** → `engineering`
     - **Asset Management** → `corporate_fare`
     - **Construction** → `construction`
   - These SVG definitions are self-contained and render instantly offline without making any network requests.

2. **Cleaned up stylesheet**:
   - Added class styling in [style.css](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/style.css) for `.material-symbol-svg` to match the exact size (`18px`) and transition behavior of Lucide SVGs, inheriting current text colors (`fill: currentColor`) perfectly.
   - Removed the obsolete Google Fonts CDN import link from [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html) to eliminate the redundant network request.

3. **Walkthrough Document Update**:
   - Documented the changes in [walkthrough.md](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/brain/e2ce67ec-d36e-4d6a-b7d1-a7103081bfc8/walkthrough.md).

---

### Verification
You can now refresh the web page in your browser. The icons will load instantly, align perfectly, and will never overflow or overlap with the sidebar text again!

1: # Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough
2: 
3: We have successfully updated the Greens Global Master Web Portal to include the exact layout, navigation structure, and KPI components of the Figma designs for the **Manager Dashboard** (2nd tab), **Tasks / Work Management** (3rd tab), **Knowledge Base** (4th tab), **IT Department** (5th tab), **Accounting** (6th tab), **Construction** (7th tab), **Development** (8th tab), the newly merged **Marketing & Reputation** (9th tab) sections, the **Website Management** (10th tab) section, the **Asset Management** (11th tab) section, the newly built **External Links** (12th tab) section, and the **Admin & Access Control** (13th tab) section.
4: 
5: Since the scripts load globally without modular compilation, you can run the entire site offline, directly from your local file system.
6: 
7: Here is a summary of the implementation details, files, and features.
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## Features Implemented
12: 
13: ### 1. Manager Dashboard (2nd Tab)
14: - **5 Summary Cards**: Displays total tasks (45), overdue tasks (5), pending action alerts (3), estimated hours (180h), and active team members (6).
15: - **Utility Buttons**: Outlined buttons for "Filters" and "Export Report".
16: - **Sub-navigation pills**: Renders selectors for "Workload by Employee", "Project-wise Tasks", "Pending Actions", and "Team Calendar".
17: - **Employee Workload Analysis**: Detailed workload logs for the 6 active employees with visual completion meters.
18: 
19: ### 2. Tasks / Work Management (3rd Tab)
20: - **Asana-integrated detailed rows**: Cards displaying task ID, title, project area, assignee, due date, estimated vs. actual hours, and the latest sub-comment.
21: - **Pill status badges**: Renders inline badges for priority (Urgent, High, Medium, Low), task status (In Progress, To Do, Needs Review, Overdue), and department, along with a dark Asana "Synced" badge.
22: - **5 Summary Cards**: Total tasks (8), in progress (4), overdue (1), no comments (3), and needs review (1).
23: - **Search & Filters**: Real-time title/assignee filtering on keypress.
24: - **New Task Modal**: Allows creating new detailed card rows dynamically.
25: 
The above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.

**Designing the Merge**

I'm now focusing on designing the merge of Website Management and IT. I've decided on the name **IT & Website Management** (or **IT & Website**). The new unified tab should follow the successful "Marketing & Reputation" model used in the sidebar.


**Refining the Implementation**

I've updated the `index.html` file to consolidate the "IT" and "Website Management" sidebar items into a single "IT & Website Management" entry. I'm keeping the "monitor" icon for now. I've also removed the separate "website" sidebar item entirely. I'm now examining the `app.js` routing logic and will adjust the `renderIT` function in views/it.js to include sub-tab navigation and render website functionality.


**Implementing the Sub-Tabs**

I've made progress implementing the sub-tabs in the IT & Website view. I've defined `state.activeITSubTab || 'integrations'` and implemented the redirection logic in `app.js` to handle `navigateTo('website')` correctly. I'm merging `views/it.js` and `views/website.js` and have added the tab navigation at the top of the file, styling it and porting styles from existing code.

"import json
import re

jsonl_path = r"C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\brain\e2ce67ec-d36e-4d6a-b7d1-a7103081bfc8\.system_generated\logs\transcript.jsonl"
out_path = r"C:\Users\DELL\.gemini\antigravity\scratch\master-construction-portal\views\asset_restored.js"

with open(jsonl_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    for line in f:
        if "views/asset.js" in line and "CodeContent" in line and "Deprecated" not in line:
            data = json.loads(line)
            for tc in data.get("tool_calls", []):
                if tc.get("name") in ["write_to_file", "write_to_file_content"]:
                    code = tc["args"]["CodeContent"]
                    # If it has surrounding double quotes and is serialized
                    if code.startswith('"') and code.endswith('"'):
                        # Using json.loads to unescape
                        try:
                            code = json.loads(code)
                        except Exception:
                            code = code[1:-1]
                    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out_f:
                        out_f.write(code)
                    print("Restored asset.js successfully to:", out_path)
                    exit(0)

print("Could not find view/asset.js creation in log")
"

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"implementation_plan","RequestFeedback":true,"Summary":"Implementation plan to undo the Asset Management and IT merge. args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Revert IT Merge & Restore Asset Management Tab

This plan details the steps to undo the merging of **Asset Management** into the IT submenu. We will restore **Asset Management** as a standalone top-level tab in the sidebar navigation and recover the independent `views/asset.js` view module.

## Proposed Changes

### Navigation & Layout

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Revert the `IT & Website Management` list item from the expandable `.has-submenu` structure back to a standard navigation tab.
- Re-insert the standalone **Asset Management** tab in the sidebar navigation list, positioned right after the **Marketing & Reputation** tab, utilizing the Google Material Symbols Outlined `corporate_fare` SVG icon.
- Re-add the script tag `<script src="views/asset.js"></script>` in the scripts load order.

### Application Routing

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)
- Revert the `case 'asset':` routing logic to call `window.renderAsset(mainViewport, state, navigateTo)` directly, rather than setting `state.activeITSubTab` and redirecting to `it`.
- Remove the IT submenu expansion/highlighting logic from the `navigateTo` router function.

### Asset View Component

#### [MODIFY] [views/asset.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/asset.js)
- Overwrite the deprecation file to restore the full Asset Management view module.
- Render the 4 KPI cards (Total Assets, Checked Out, Available, Overdue) with dynamic baseline offset calculations to match the original numbers:

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Merge Asset Management into IT with Sidebar Chevron Dropdown

We will merge **Asset Management** into the **IT & Website Management** section. Beside the IT tab, we will implement a chevron button that toggles a vertical submenu list of the three IT subtabs. When the menu is expanded, the down-arrow changes to an up-arrow, which collapses the menu when clicked. Clicking any sub-tab loads the view and highlights the tab.

## Proposed Changes

### Stylesheets

#### [MODIFY] [style.css](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/style.css)
- Style `.nav-item.has-submenu` to lay out the main item and submenu vertically.
- Style `.nav-item-main` as a full-width flex container.
- Style `.submenu-toggle-btn` to render the chevron down/up toggler.
- Style `.sidebar-submenu` to display subtabs in a vertical line with indentation.
- Style `.submenu-item` to match standard sidebar hover and active states.
- Style `.asset-status-checked-out`, `.asset-status-available`, and `.asset-status-overdue` badges for asset rows.

### Sidebar UI

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Ensure the IT navigation element `#it-nav-item` is structured as a parent item `.has-submenu`.
- Verify the toggle button `#it-submenu-toggle` is styled with standard Lucide icon placeholder.
- Add the vertical submenu `ul` showing three submenu items:
  1. System Integrations (`integrations`)
  2. Website Management (`website`)
  3. Asset Management (`asset`)

### Sidebar Controller & Router

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)
- Wire up the click event handler for `#it-submenu-toggle` to expand/collapse the vertical list and dynamically flip the Lucide icon between `chevron-down` and `chevron-up`.
- Wire up click event handlers for `.submenu-item` elements to load the corresponding sub-view inside the unified IT view.

"# Greens Global - Web Portal Walkthrough: IT & Asset Management Merge

We have successfully merged the **Asset Management** dashboard into the unified **IT & Website Management** section, and implemented a vertical sidebar dropdown submenu with a dynamic chevron toggler.

---

## Technical Implementations

### 1. Sidebar Submenu & Toggler
- **Structure (`index.html`)**: 
  - Restructured the **IT & Website Management** navigation link into a `.has-submenu` list item.
  - Placed the text label, main icon, and a `.submenu-toggle-btn` containing a `chevron-down` icon inside a `.nav-item-main` row.
  - Added a nested `ul.sidebar-submenu` list with three vertical subtabs:
    1. **System Integrations** (`integrations`)
    2. **Website Management** (`website`)
    3. **Asset Management** (`asset`)
- **Styling (`style.css`)**:
  - Overrode standard `.nav-item` layout rules when combined with `.has-submenu` to align items vertically.
  - Designed the `.sidebar-submenu` vertical list container with clear indentation and spacing.
  - Customized the `.submenu-toggle-btn` to fit into the navigation bar, supporting hover effects.
- **Controller Logic (`app.js`)**:
  - Wired a click listener to the chevron toggle button to expand/collapse the submenu list.
  - Automatically flipped the chevron to `chevron-up` when the menu is expanded, and `chevron-down` when collapsed.
  - Configured `navigateTo('it')` to auto-expand the submenu and set the chevron to `chevron-up` whenever entering the IT section, and auto-collapse the menu when navigating to other views.
  - Bound click listeners to each `.submenu-item` to set `state.activeITSubTab` and redirect to the unified IT view.

### 2. Unified Asset Management UI (`views/it.js`)
- **Tab Swapping**: Added the **Asset Management** switcher button in the unified header navigation panel of `views/it.js` using inlined offline SVGs.
- **KPI Dashboards**: Implemented the four asset status cards (Total, Checked Out, Available, Overdue) with the dynamic offset ca

args.CodeContent:
"# Implementation Plan - Web Portal Upgrade (IT, Knowledge Base, Accounting)

We will upgrade the portal's **IT**, **Knowledge Base**, and **Accounting** sections. We will implement new sub-tab switchers, expand the sidebar navigation, and add interactive dashboards, forms, and workflows.

---

## Proposed Changes

### Global Configurations & Seeding

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)
- **State Expansion**: Seed new array lists for:
  - `itAccessAccounts`: IT accounts list (Name, System Role, VPN status, MFA).
  - `itNetworkNodes`: Network infrastructure switches, routers, and firewalls.
  - `lmsCourses`: Learning courses list (Title, time, progress, status).
  - `rampTransactions`: Ramp corporate card logs, highlighting missing fields (Memos).
  - `amaEntities`: Entity Agreement Fee records.
- **Route Enhancements**:
  - Add router cases for the new sub-views (`access`, `integrations`, `network`, `sop-index`, `sop-review`, `lms`) to direct navigation properly.
  - Set default state: `state.activeITSubTab = 'access'` and `state.activeSopSubTab = 'index'`.

### Sidebar UI

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Rename the parent tab **IT & Website Management** to **IT**.
- Update the nested vertical `ul.sidebar-submenu` list under the IT tab to display the 5 new submenu links:
  1. Access Management (`access`)
  2. Website Management (`website`)
  3. API & Integrations (`integrations`)
  4. Hardware Assets (`asset`)
  5. Network Management (`network`)

---

### Module-Specific Implementations

### 1. IT Section

#### [MODIFY] [views/it.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/views/it.js)
- Update `renderIT` header switcher to show all 5 tabs.
- **Access Management Tab** (`access`):
  - Renders 4 KPI cards: Active Accounts, MFA Status, Active VPNs, API Keys.
  - Renders accounts tabl

"# Task Checklist - Portal Upgrade (IT, Knowledge Base, Accounting)

- [ ] Seeding and Router Updates (`app.js`)
  - [ ] Add state lists for `itAccessAccounts`, `itNetworkNodes`, `lmsCourses`, `rampTransactions`, and `amaEntities`
  - [ ] Add routing cases for the new sub-views in the navigation controller
  - [ ] Set default `state.activeITSubTab = 'access'` and `state.activeSopSubTab = 'index'`
- [ ] Sidebar Layout Upgrades (`index.html`)
  - [ ] Rename the parent tab **IT & Website Management** to **IT**
  - [ ] Update the nested `ul.sidebar-submenu` list to show Access, Website, API & Integrations, Hardware Assets, and Network Management
- [ ] IT Section Upgrades (`views/it.js`)
  - [ ] Update the switcher header inside `renderIT` to render all 5 tabs
  - [ ] Implement `Access Management` panel layout and CRUD event handlers
  - [ ] Rename `System Integrations` references to `API & Integrations`
  - [ ] Rename `Asset Management` references to `Hardware Assets`
  - [ ] Implement `Network Management` panel layout and ping event handlers
- [ ] Knowledge Base Section Upgrades (`views/sop.js`)
  - [ ] Add a sub-tab switcher for SOP Index, Review SOP, and LMS
  - [ ] Implement `Review SOP` list with approval status workflow
  - [ ] Implement `LMS` training dashboard course grid and onboarding status
- [ ] Accounting Section Upgrades (`views/accounting.js`)
  - [ ] Add a scrollable tab switcher container supporting 7 tabs
  - [ ] Implement `Import Hub` uploader interface for Fidelity, QuickBooks Payroll, and Tally (GG Con)
  - [ ] Implement `Ramp Cards` uploader showing missing memos and inline memo editing
  - [ ] Implement `AMA Entities` fee logs tracking
  - [ ] Implement budget adjustment modal form
  - [ ] Implement `Reports` ledger downloads
- [ ] Verify Functionality
  - [ ] Load the page and verify all new tabs and nested subtabs function correctly
  - [ ] Verify new interactive forms and modals operate without console warnings
"

The approach mirrors the functionality in `views/it.js`. It's very clean. I'm also adding styling for a scrollable tab list for the 7 tabs using flexbox and overflow-x. Currently, the scrollable tab list includes "Transactions," "Invoices," "Budgets," "Import Hub," "Ramp Cards," "AMA Entities," and "Reports."
I've started designing the "Import Hub" tab's panel, which will include specific sections for Fidelity, QuickBooks Payroll, and Tally (GG Con), each with a simulated file uploader and progress indicator.


**Designing Import Hub Interactions**

I'm now focused on the "Import Hub" tab's interactions. The plan is to create a seamless user experience for importing data. I'm building out a progress bar that loads from 0% to 100% in 1.5 seconds. Upon completion, it displays a success message, such as "Import complete. Synced [N] ledger rows to Sage Intacct database," and records the activity in the g
<truncated 4029 bytes>
 bars for each service. I'm focusing on ensuring the visual styling aligns with the overall design. I'm also ensuring the "Ramp Cards" tab is rendering and working as designed. I have finished adding the content to all the tabs.


**Refining Ramp Card Display**

I've been improving the display for the "Ramp Cards" tab, specifically the missing memo notifications. I've enhanced the visual indicators for transactions with missing memos by adding a count of missing memos and styling each transaction row with a warning background. I am also implementing the input field and save button for adding memos.


**Refining AMA Tab Display**

I'm now polishing the "AMA Entities" tab's display, focusing on visual clarity. I've designed and integrated a table to present essential data for each entity, including their agreement status, fee rates, and year-to-date billing. I've added a total billed YTD summary, styled as a highlight, and the design now features a modal for creating new entity agreements.


**Refining AMA Form**

I'm now integrating the "AMA Entities" modal, focusing on form enhancements. I've built the HTML for the modal with fields for entity name, fee rate, and status selection and the cancel and create buttons. The form is now working to capture the relevant information.


**Implementing Report Downloads**

I'm now integrating the download functionality for reports within the "Reports" tab, including "Profit & Loss" and "Balance Sheet". I've created the PDF and CSV download buttons, along with a simulated loading indicator. I'm focusing on user feedback for download success. I'm also ensuring the correct reports are being rendered and downloaded.


**Finalizing Report Tab**

I'm now integrating a Capital Expenditures report within the "Reports" tab. I've designed a styled card for the report, complete with a download button, as shown in the example provided. I'm focusing on ensuring its functionality, appearance, and alignment with the report style. I'm also ensuring that the budgets data renders correctly.

args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Web Operations Portal: Comprehensive Upgrades Walkthrough

We have successfully implemented the comprehensive portal upgrades across the **IT**, **Knowledge Base**, and **Accounting** departments. 

---

## Technical & Functional Upgrades

### 1. IT Department View
- **Tab Layout & Switcher**: Expanded the sub-tab navigation to support five sections:
  1. **Access Management** (`access`): Renders KPI cards (Active Accounts, MFA Status, Active VPNs, API Keys) and an employee credentials list. Features "+ Register Credentials" modal form, "Rotate Keyring" simulator, and "Revoke All VPNs" profile reset.
  2. **Website Management** (`website`): Retains domain uptime monitors and register/edit website configurations.
  3. **API & Integrations** (`integrations`): Displays connected services with dynamic status bars. Features a new **"+ Generate API Key"** token generation tool.
  4. **Hardware Assets** (`asset`): Tracks assigned physical hardware (formerly *Asset Management*) with a catalog editing/deletion interface.
  5. **Network Management** (`network`): Displays status cards and lists network infrastructure node ping times. Features a **"Ping Network Nodes"** request simulator and **"Scan IP Range"** subnet scan simulator.

### 2. Knowledge Base Department View
- **Tab Switcher Layout**: Added a top navigation pill switcher containing three views:
  1. **SOP Index** (`index`): The core categories directory and search bar, supporting "+ New SOP" document creation.
  2. **Review SOP** (`review`): Tracks documents in "Under Review" state. Features interactive **"Approve & Publish"** buttons to immediately update document status and sync them back to the active index.
  3. **LMS Compliance** (`lms`): Displays training courses. Features interactive **"Study Lesson"** progress increments (+20% per study session) that dynamically mark courses as "Completed" upon hitting 100%, and a "+ Register Course" catalog manager.

### 3. Accounting Department View
- *
<truncated 3500 bytes>

"# Implementation Plan - Sidebar Dropdown and IT/Network Refinements

We will implement the sidebar vertical dropdown list for the **Knowledge Base** section, remove the **Register Asset** button in the Hardware Assets sub-tab, and rebuild the **Network Management** tab to represent a dedicated **UniFi Controller Integration Dashboard**.

---

## Proposed Changes

### 1. Sidebar Submenu for Knowledge Base

#### [MODIFY] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/index.html)
- Restructure the **Knowledge Base** menu item (`data-view="sop"`) into a collapsible dropdown list similar to the IT tab.
- Insert a `.submenu-toggle-btn` containing a `chevron-down` icon.
- Add a nested `ul.sidebar-submenu` list for:
  - **SOP Index** (`index`)
  - **Review SOP** (`review`)
  - **LMS** (`lms`)

#### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/DELL/.gemini/antigravity/scratch/master-construction-portal/app.js)

args.CodeContent:
"# Greens Global - Operations Portal Walkthrough: Sidebar Dropdown and IT/Network Refinements

We have successfully implemented a vertical sidebar dropdown submenu for the **Knowledge Base** section, removed the "+ Register Asset" button from **Hardware Assets**, and replaced the old network monitor tab with a dedicated **UniFi Controller Integration Dashboard**.

---

## Technical Implementations

### 1. Sidebar Collapsible Submenu for Knowledge Base
- **Layout Restructure (`index.html`)**:
  - Converted the single **Knowledge Base** nav item to support the collapsible submenu list structure, containing down/up arrow chevrons.
  - Added three vertical subtabs:
    1. **SOP Index** (`index`)
    2. **Review SOP** (`review`)
    3. **LMS** (`lms`)
- **Controller Logic (`app.js`)**:
  - Enhanced the click listener to dynamically check the clicked submenu's parent view:
    - If parent is `it`, update `state.activeITSubTab` and route to the IT view.
    - If parent is `sop`, update `state.activeSopSubTab` and route to the Knowledge Base view.
  - Integrated auto-expansion/collapse logic inside `navigateTo(viewName)`. Entering Knowledge Base expands its submenu and flips the chevron up; leaving collapses the menu and flips the chevron down.

### 2. IT Refinements (`views/it.js`)
- **Hardware Assets View**: Removed the "+ Register Asset" button from the sub-tab header. Added safety checks inside `attachAssetHandlers` to verify the button element exists before trying to register click listeners.
- **UniFi Integration View (Network Management)**: Replaced the old network tables with a **UniFi Controller Integration Dashboard**:
  - **KPIs**: Live connection status, count of online devices, client telemetry, and average network experience score.
  - **UniFi Devices Table**: Lists gateways, access switches, and access points.
  - **Restart AP Simulator**: Clicking "Restart" on any AP changes its status to "Rebooting...", triggers an API call simulation, and changes Adopted status back to 
<truncated 1819 bytes>

"# Greens Global Portal - Comprehensive Upgrades Walkthrough

We have successfully implemented the comprehensive portal upgrades requested by the boss. Below is the technical breakdown of the implemented features, files added/modified, and instructions on how to manually verify them in the portal.

---

## 1. Global Navigation & Collapsible Submenus (`index.html`, `app.js`)
- **Collapsible Sidebar Dropdowns**: Converted all new and expanded departments into collapsible dropdown menus inside `index.html`. All submenus use unified chevron-up/down icon triggers, active-subtab highlights, and vertical layouts matching the existing IT and SOP sections.
- **Auto-Expanding/Collapsing Router (`app.js`)**: Rewrote the sidebar navigation engine inside `navigateTo`. When navigating to a specific subtab, its parent sidebar folder automatically expands, flips its chevron up, highlights the active subtab, and collapses all other sidebar sections.
- **Registered Routes**: Added routing cases and initialized state values for all new subtabs.

---

## 2. New Integrated Departments

### Human Resources (`views/hr.js`)
- **Microsoft AD Onboarding (`hr-ms`)**: Displays new hires and AD account creation statuses. Clicking **"Push to Microsoft AD"** connects to a simulated MS Graph API, animates a loading spinner, provisions the account/mailbox, and updates its active telemetry status.
- **Asana Onboarding Checklist (`hr-asana`)**: Displays onboarding task items synced from Asana. Clicking **"Mark Complete"** updates the status in the local state, and clicking **"Sync with Asana API"** performs a simulated synchronization.
- **Zoning & Corporate Disclosures (`hr-disclosures`)**: Tracks signed NDA and Conflict of Interest disclosures. Clicking **"View"** loads the document from a simulated secure vault.
- **Documents Directory (`hr-documents`)**: Central storage containing handbooks, benefits guides, tax forms, and direct deposit forms with simulation download triggers.

### Property Asset Management (`view

"# Execution Checklist - Greens Global Portal Upgrades

We will track the implementation of the new sections and tabs in the portal as detailed in the approved implementation plan.

---

## 1. Global Layout & Routing Configuration
- [x] Restructure sidebar navigation to support collapsible submenus for all new/modified sections (`index.html`)
  - [x] Construction / Operations dropdown subtabs
  - [x] Development dropdown subtabs
  - [x] Asset Management dropdown subtabs
  - [x] HR dropdown subtabs
  - [x] Investor Relations dropdown subtabs
- [x] Initialize state datasets, navigation switch cases, and auto-expanding menu handlers (`app.js`)
  - [x] Add seed data arrays for onboarding, permits, warranties, inspections, investor portfolios
  - [x] Connect submenu click events and write navigation router logic
  - [x] Add route switch cases for all subviews

---

## 2. New Views Creation
- [x] Build HR operations dashboard (`views/hr.js`)
  - [x] Microsoft AD Account provisioning view & simulation
  - [x] Asana Onboarding Checklist integrations
  - [x] Legal disclosures & NDA signings log
  - [x] HR documents vault
- [x] Build Property Asset Management dashboard (`views/propertyAsset.js`)
  - [x] Property Portfolio specs
  - [x] Mechanical/HVAC Equipment Warranties log
  - [x] As-Built final CAD designs vault
  - [x] Annual Inspections & Certifications table
- [x] Build Investor Relations dashboard (`views/investorRelations.js`)
  - [x] Investor dashboard with equity distributions, funding rounds, KPIs
  - [x] Investor reports & financial prospectus downloads

---

## 3. Existing Views Upgrades
- [x] Expand Operations View with Cubby Integration (`views/operations.js`)
  - [x] Folder explorer browser simulation
  - [x] Vault sync status logs
  - [x] Add/upload drawings simulation
- [x] Expand Development View (`views/development.js`)
  - [x] Project Directory (existing)
  - [x] Permit Status table & submissions tracker
  - [x] Design plans & MEP blueprints by project
  -

I've noted three key integrations listed: Fidelity Investments, QuickBooks Payroll, and Tally (GG Con). There's an immediate interest, and I am investigating why MRE and MRI, real estate management software platforms, were not included in the list. **Adding New Financial Integrations** I've determined that MRE and MRI should be added as import cards within the Financial Import Hub, which makes perfect sense. I've located the Financial Import Hub page in `views/accounting.js` and confirmed it renders a grid of import cards. I'm considering using `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(280px, 1fr))` to allow the layout to automatically wrap and scale. I am deciding on the title, icon and descriptions for both software integrations. For MRE, I am leaning toward "MRE Real Estate Accounting" with the `building` icon. **Refining Card Implementation** I'm now zeroing in on the finer points of integrating MRE and MRI. I've chosen "MRI Property Software" with the `server` icon, and the color scheme matches. The description is set. The JavaScript code confirms the "import-trigger" class and the `data-service` attributes are being utilized correctly. The dynamic handling in the code is confirmed to be compatible, using the `cost: service === 'Fidelity' ? ... : ...` methodology. I'm now fine-tuning how the new cards will be wrapped in the grid layout, with responsive design. I've also verified the `tabName` conditions for "imports" are properly rendering. **Implementing Sidebar Dividers** I'm now integrating subtle horizontal dividers into the sidebar. I've updated the financial import hub description and grid, and confirmed the layout updates. I've analyzed the sidebar's `index.html` structure and identified the navigation items to divide, noting specific requirements for styling and responsive behavior. I'm focusing on balancing visual separation with a clean design, ensuring dividers a SaaS dashboards. I'm now testing these dividers. **Adding Navigation Dividers** I've determined that inserting a custom `
  • ` element between navigation items is cleanest. Implementing this divider, styled with a subtle `border-bottom`, aligns perfectly with the sidebar's design. This method avoids affecting existing styles and is easy to maintain. I've successfully met the requirement of adding a divider below the "NAVIGATION" section. **Implementing Section Dividers** I'm now focusing on integrating section dividers throughout the sidebar, as requested. I've decided to add a `
    ` element after each major department section title, replicating the same divider that I used for navigation. I've confirmed that this approach aligns with the design guidelines and is the cleanest way to implement the desired visual separation between sections. **Confirming Divider Placement** I've confirmed I can cleanly integrate section dividers in the sidebar. Adding a `
    ` after each major section title, matching the navigation divider style, is confirmed. The approach is validated and consistent. I've also verified that the existing JavaScript, particularly the `.nav-item` selector, won't be affected by the added divider element, so the performance and stability are preserved. **Optimizing Divider Styling** I'm now refining the divider styling, and I'm very pleased with the current performance. I am confident that the custom `
    ` implementation, utilizing `border-bottom`, aligns perfectly with the sidebar's overall design. I've confirmed that the `.nav-item` selector in the JavaScript code completely ignores the divider, which proves that the design is robust. I've also verified that using `background-color: var(--border-color); opacity: 0.6;` delivers excellent results in both light and dark modes, ensuring a consistent premium look, and will keep the implementation easy to maintain.

My focus is now on discerning *why* they want the changes reverted – it seems they felt I misunderstood their initial request. I'm revisiting the specific instructions from the prior step.

I've realized a critical misunderstanding of the "MRE" and "MRI" requests. They weren't meant as cards within the "Financial Import Hub," but as sub-tabs under "Accounting" in the sidebar, mirroring the IT and other examples. The earlier attempts to add them as cards clearly missed the mark, hence the user's feedback. I am now focused on incorporating the dropdown arrow in the sidebar, horizontal sub-tabs, and new views based on user requirements. My next steps involve adding the needed divider lines in the sidebar.
